overrides.override_summary: Post-term covenant not to compete is void under Business and Professions Code Section 16600
overrides.why_it_matters: CA franchisees can operate a competing business immediately after franchise ends — noncompete provision is unenforceable

overrides.override_summary: Liquidated damages clause may be unenforceable under Civil Code Section 1671
overrides.why_it_matters: The $15,000 + 8% noncompete violation penalty may not be enforceable in CA

overrides.override_summary: General release must not waive rights under Franchise Investment Law (Corp Code §31512) or Franchise Relations Act (B&P Code §20010)
overrides.why_it_matters: Renewal/transfer release cannot extinguish CA franchise law claims

overrides.override_summary: Connecticut arbitration venue may not be enforceable under CA law. CA Franchise Investment Law and Franchise Relations Act apply regardless of choice of law or venue.
overrides.why_it_matters: CA franchisees may be able to arbitrate/litigate in California rather than Connecticut

overrides.override_summary: Florida choice of law provision may not be enforceable for CA franchisees
overrides.why_it_matters: CA law may govern rather than FL law for CA-based disputes

overrides.override_summary: No statement or acknowledgment shall waive claims under applicable state franchise law or disclaim reliance on franchisor statements
overrides.why_it_matters: Protects franchisees from signing away fraud-in-the-inducement claims

overrides.override_summary: No statement or acknowledgment shall waive claims under applicable state franchise law or disclaim reliance
Section 4 of IFDA provides jurisdiction in Illinois for IL franchisees
overrides.why_it_matters: IL franchisees may have right to litigate in IL courts

overrides.override_summary: Arbitration may be conducted in Connecticut OR Maryland
overrides.why_it_matters: MD franchisees have choice of venue including their home state

overrides.override_summary: General release shall not apply to liability under Maryland Franchise Registration and Disclosure Law. Excludes alleged breach under Franchise Law.
overrides.why_it_matters: MD franchisees retain right to sue under state franchise law despite signing release

overrides.override_summary: Any limitation on time to bring claims shall not reduce the 3-year statute of limitations under MD Franchise Law
overrides.why_it_matters: Franchisees have full 3 years to bring claims regardless of FA limitations

overrides.override_summary: Sections 25.A-25.F of FA deleted; no waiver of MD franchise law rights
overrides.why_it_matters: Comprehensive protection of franchisee rights under state law

overrides.override_summary: 90 days notice of termination (with 60 days to cure) and 180 days notice for nonrenewal required under Minn. Stat. Sec. 80C.14
overrides.why_it_matters: Substantially more cure time than FA's 10/30 day periods; 180-day nonrenewal notice vs. FA's 12-month renewal notice

overrides.override_summary: Release cannot relieve liability under Minnesota Statutes Chapter 80C
overrides.why_it_matters: MN franchise law claims survive any general release

overrides.override_summary: Right to submit matters to jurisdiction of MN courts preserved
overrides.why_it_matters: MN franchisees retain court access in their home state

overrides.override_summary: Post-term covenants not to compete may be subject to limitations under ND law
overrides.why_it_matters: Noncompete may be unenforceable or limited in ND

overrides.override_summary: Connecticut venue and Florida choice of law may be unenforceable
overrides.why_it_matters: ND franchisees may be able to litigate locally under ND law

overrides.override_summary: Virginia Retail Franchising Act provisions apply
overrides.why_it_matters: Additional franchisee protections under state law

overrides.override_summary: Release or waiver provisions may be void under RCW 19.100
overrides.why_it_matters: WA franchise law protections cannot be waived
